steps.title: Spot the defining relationship
steps.content: The key signal in the passage is the statement that “the modern alpaca gets only 64 percent of its genetic material from its wild ancestor, the vicuña, and the rest from the domesticated llama”. This directly establishes a genetic transfer from the llama to the alpaca during their coexistence.

steps.title: Match the logical inference
steps.content: Choice C aligns with this genetic mixing by stating that the alpaca's domestication process involved adding genetic material from the llama. A, B, and D misinterpret the scope of the findings. A incorrectly links genetic diversity to percentages; B misreads the domestication timeline; D introduces unmentioned wild interbreeding. That matches choice C — C is correct.
